note: New shared core::project::deletion::delete_node (crates/core/src/core/project/deletion.rs) has correct ordering: walk subtree → delete files via writer::delete_document (inheriting R-14 safe-path) → only THEN prune hierarchy + document map. On mid-operation filesystem error, the loaded Project is structurally intact so the caller doesn't write a smaller manifest. Test delete_folder_removes_descendant_files_and_prevents_repair_resurrection covers the exact threat: delete folder with nested doc, assert .md + .meta gone, document map pruned, and R-19's reader-repair doesn't bring it back. Linux bridge now calls the shared helper and clears active editor for deleted descendants. Old hierarchy-only remove helper deleted so behavior can't drift back. Linux Qt host validation acceptable gap (macOS cxx-qt environmental failure, not project code). Tauri duplicate is follow-up unification.
